page: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.8xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. The CPU architecture is x86_64, utilizing hyper-threading with two threads per core. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 with VNNI and BF16 support, alongside Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for accelerated matrix operations. In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong performance in single-threaded gzip compression at 32.42 GB/sec, Geekbench single-core tasks at 2023.0, and image and text processing. Multi-core performance, such as stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores, falls within the average range.

The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers a strong write bandwidth for large blocks at 89.67 GB/sec, while read bandwidth remains average. Static web serving throughput is strong at 48.18 GB/sec. For machine learning workloads, the instance achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 332.68 tokens/sec on a small 135M LLM model, though performance drops to average for 7B models and is weak for large 70B models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m7i-flex.8xlarge offers a balanced resource density for general-purpose workloads that require dedicated CPU allocation and modern DDR5 memory without the overhead of local NVMe storage. It is well-suited for web serving, application hosting, software compilation, database operations, and moderate machine learning inference tasks that leverage Intel AMX instructions.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, running on an Intel Xeon 8488C processor via the Nitro hypervisor. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs but offers a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. The processor supports AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions, yielding strong performance in single-threaded gzip compression, static web serving, and text generation for small LLM models. Multi-core CPU and memory read benchmarks perform in the average tier. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated compute resources and high-speed memory without local storage needs, making it suitable for web serving, database operations, and light machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 128.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering balanced performance for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 32 vCPUs, 128.0 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed DDR5 memory, and advanced matrix acceleration for versatile workloads.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Intel Xeon 8488C processor with AVX-512 and AMX extensions
Strong performance in static web serving and single-threaded compression
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor orchestration
Suited for web serving, database operations, and moderate LLM inference
